# SL-HEAD-006: h1-rendered-only

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

The H1 exists only after JavaScript rendering.

## Metadata

- Category: Headings and structure (headings-structure)
- Default severity: warning
- Confidence: likely
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- raw-html
- rendered-dom

## Required Evidence

- raw-heading-state
- rendered-heading-state

## When This Triggers

Compare H1 presence in raw HTML and rendered DOM.

## False Positives

False positives are expected to be rare because this rule relies on
deterministic local evidence. Suppress only with a reason when the route
contract intentionally differs from the observed signal.

## How To Fix

Render primary headings in the initial HTML for indexable pages.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/headings/h1-rendered-only

Fixed direction:

- Render primary headings in the initial HTML for indexable pages.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
